source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Water flows steadily from an open tank. The cross-sectional area at point 2 is \( 0.0480\, \text{m}^2 \); at point 3 it is \( 0.0160\, \text{m}^2 \). The area of the tank is very large compared with the cross-sectional area of the pipe. Assuming that Bernoulli’s equation applies, compute the gauge pressure at point 2.

multi_step: Solving for gauge pressure at point 2 under Bernoulli typically requires combining continuity and Bernoulli relations.

The problem involves multiple labeled locations in the system (tank/open surface, point 2, point 3), suggesting at least two or more reasoning steps.
verifiability: The expected answer is a single numeric value in pressure units (answer_type=short_text, answer_text='69700Pa').
There are 4 multiple-choice options, which makes final-answer checking straightforward.
Given the diagram and stated assumptions, the solution can be checked against standard fluid mechanics relations; registry_passed=true supports asset completeness.
